lastReleaseMessage: v0.13.2: SQLCipher key-escrow doc + round-trip test; keytar-migration backlog item; .finances history-scrub script (dry-run default); drop tracked .bak; commit 06-10 doc/dashboard batch.

barRaise.topFinding: The dominant story is stable and now well-characterized: a 13-file release cohort has been ship-ready since 06-12 and is waiting on one human release.ps1 command, not broken. This run's genuine progress is that last cycle's one new code regression already closed in the working tree: release.ps1 no longer auto-stubs the CHANGELOG, since a version bump without -Message now errors instead of synthesizing a file-count entry. The finding that deserves escalation is the oldest one on the board: real bank statements still sit in pushed git history (.finances/ at 44b7b10, f670d17, 43ea16b on the GitHub remote), a direct violation of the project's defining invariant, carried across seven audits and only this week finally filed as a P0 whose queue write was declined and awaits approval. Two cosmetic residuals from the rename cohort surfaced: docs/landing-page.html still reads YesAndBudget 16 times, and two untracked .bak orphans accreted in scripts/. The single highest-value action is unchanged and boring: ship the prepared cohort, then run the history scrub.
